entry.title: Re: How to create a DB connection to HANA Data Lake in SL...
entry.content:
I'm specifically noting this error: ERROR:

The chain of certificates is incomplete or untrusted, missing certificate of [C1:AD:77:78] CN=DigiCert SHA2 Secure Server CA, O=DigiCert Inc, C=US # --- Summary ----------- #01 Certificate (End Entity): VALID Subject: CN=hanacloud.ondemand.com, O=SAP SE, L=Walldorf, SP=Baden-WÃ¼rttemberg, C=DE Issuer: CN=DigiCert SHA2 Secure Server CA, O=DigiCert Inc, C=US Fingerprint (SHA256):

There is something it doesn't like about that certificate. Keep in mind that a certificate chain can be multiple levels deep. The most likely explanation  is that there is an intermediate certificate that you need to include that was used between the DigiCert root and the final certificate. 

You might also want to try replacing the "Ã¼"  in the "SP=Baden-WÃ¼rttemberg" field. I expect that was original "Baden-Württemberg" and it is getting misinterpreted somewhere. While the certificate should be able to handle the special character, it is worth ruling it out as an issue.

entry.title: How to create custom catalogs and target mapping
entry.content:
Is there any detailed steps on how we can create a custom catalog and group as well as the target mapping?

addition to our query, may we know how to delete the published variant in UI?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to create custom catalogs and target mapping
entry.content:
Creating Custom Fiori Catalogs and Groups

To create a custom catalog and group in SAP Fiori Launchpad, as well as set up target mapping, follow these steps:

  1. Open the Fiori Launchpad Designer using transaction /ui2/flpd_conf

  2. To create a new catalog:

    • Identify an existing catalog to use as a template or create a new one
    • If creating new, name it appropriately (e.g., YOUR_BC_QLTY_EVAL_OTC)

  3. To create a new group:

    • In the Launchpad Designer, select the "Groups" section.
    • Choose "Create" to add a new group.
    • Provide a title and ID for the group.

  4. For target mapping:

    • Open the source catalog containing the tiles you want to reference (e.g., SAP_TC_CMD_QLTY_EVAL_COMMON)
    • Display the tiles in list format
    • Select the tile you want to add (e.g., Validation Rules)
    • Choose "Create Reference"
    • Select your custom catalog (e.g., YOUR_BC_QLTY_EVAL_OTC) as the target

  5. Repeat the process for other tiles you want to include in your custom catalog.

  6. Assign the custom catalog to the appropriate business roles (PFCG roles) to make it available to users.

 

Deleting Published Variants in UI

To delete a published variant in SAP Fiori:

  1. Open the app variant you want to delete

  2. Choose "Adapt UI" from the user actions menu

  3. If there are multiple versions, select the version you want to delete from the version history

  4. Delete the app variant from the test system first

  5. After deletion in the test system, your administrator needs to transport the deletion to the productive system

This process ensures that the variant is properly removed from both test and productive environments

entry.title: Email Notification Issue for Rejected/Partially Rejected payment batchs.
entry.content:
How to handle rejected payments and partially rejected payments and trigger and notification to team members?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Email Notification Issue for Rejected/Partially Rejec...
entry.content:
Find the Rejected Payment Workflow Management Overview:-

1. Application Usage
   Manage Workflows - For Rejected Payments: Utilized to manage workflows associated with payment documents that have been rejected during the approval process or by the bank.
   My Inbox - Process Rejected Payments: Used for reviewing the rejected payment documents during the approval process or by the bank.

2. Event Triggering and Notifications:- 
   Rejected Payment Batch: Triggers the Situation Template "FIN_BCM_BATCH_REJECTED" to notify designated team members regarding payment batches rejected by the bank.
   Partially Rejected Payment Batch: Triggers the Situation Template "FIN_BCM_BATCH_PARTIALLY_REJECTED" to inform relevant team members of payment batches that were partially rejected by bank.

 

Please find the details given in the SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3588765

entry.title: Re: SAP EBS: Question on Interpretation Algorithm "20 020...
entry.content:
@joerg_rolapp @Anita_Ahmadpour 

Hi all, I would like to give you an update. I was able to resolve my issue with posting rule algorithm "20 020" not working properly. 

Like I have described in my post, the posting rule consisted from two parts. The ex-colleague who set it this way used Posting Area 1 and Posting Area 2 even though both were posting to GL-accounts (step 1: DR bank clearing a/c CR bank, step 2: DR empty CR bank clearing a/c). I personally prefer to only use Posting Area 2 for posting to a subledger account (as recommended by SAP), but I decided to leave the posting rule as is. And so when the posting rule algorithm "20 020 Document Number Search" was not finding an open document I assumed it was because of the way the posting rule was set up with step 1 and step 2. So I have changed it to just contain one step in Posting Area 1: DR empty CR bank, with posting type 4 (clear debit GL a/c).

To my surprise, this revised posting rule with "20 020" algorithm still did not work so I started checking every possible setting. That is when I found that I was missing a correct number range for "BELNR Document Number" in t-code FEB_IMP_SOURCE (Posting Parameters). Essentially, the algorithm did not know in which number range it needed to be looking for an open document and so my posting rule was not working. After maintaining correct number range for each housebank accounts in t-code FEB_IMP_SOURCE I was able to get my posting rule working as intended.

I hope this explanation can help somebody in the future 🙂

entry.title: Re: Rating ends with error which indicate wrong Charge wa...
entry.content:
Hello Dominic,
 
As previously explained, it is not recommended to use a No Access component in recurring rates because it prevents the correct execution of the activation process, responsible for generating one-time and recurring fees.
 
Then, when Convergent Charging receives a chargeable item/CIT, it first checks if the one-time and recurring events were already triggered for the contract up to the consumption date. This ensures events are processed in the correct order and counters have the right values. If the events were not triggered, it triggers all one-time and recurring events up to the consumption date before processing the chargeable item/CIT and executing the pricing logic from the usage rate. In addition, in that case, since activation and usage rating occur in the same transaction, if the activation fails, an error is raised before the usage rating can be executed.
 
This is likely why you observed the recurring charge was executed but not the usage charge.
 
Hope this helps.
 
Best regards,
Sébastien

Hi @Bohdan, I would like to give you an update about my question. I was able to resolve my issue with posting rule algorithm "20 020 Document number search" not working properly. 

My posting rule consisted of two parts. The ex-colleague who set it this way used Posting Area 1 and Posting Area 2 even though both were posting to GL-accounts (step 1: DR bank clearing a/c CR bank, step 2: DR empty CR bank clearing a/c, Posting Type 4). I personally prefer to only use Posting Area 2 for posting to a subledger account, but I decided to leave the posting rule as is. And so when the posting rule algorithm "20 020 Document number search" was not finding an open document I assumed it was because of the way the posting rule was set up with step 1 and step 2. So I have changed it to just contain one step in Posting Area 1: DR empty CR bank, Posting Type 4.

To my surprise, this revised posting rule with "20 020 Document number search" algorithm still did not work so I started checking every possible setting. That is when I found that I was missing a correct number range for "BELNR Document Number" in t-code FEB_IMP_SOURCE (Posting Parameters). Essentially, the algorithm did not know in which number range it needed to be looking for an open document and so my posting rule was not working. After maintaining correct number range for each housebank accounts in t-code FEB_IMP_SOURCE I was able to get my posting rule working as intended.

I hope this explanation can help somebody in the future 🙂

entry.title: Create SAP Build Apps applications in minutes with AI
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog post we are covering the newly released AI feature to automatically create pages based on your existing OData backend with SAP Build Apps. This new feature provides a jump start into application development and enables you to easily extend your application based on the generated pages.

 

Prerequisites

  • Subscribed to SAP Build Apps either via your preferred payment model or trial
  • You have an existing backend based on OData, e.g., your connected SAP S/4HANA system

 

Use Case

In our example, we want to provide our field colleagues with some information about the product so that they have the information directly at hand once on the customer side.

 

Accessing SAP Build

The starting point for all SAP Build products is the lobby. In the lobby you have a holistic overview of all projects you have access to as well as additional functionalities such as accessing connectors, the SAP Build store, monitoring capabilities, and the control tower to execute additional configurations based on your tenant.

 

Enabling AI Features

To initially enable the AI features of SAP Build in your tenant, you need to enable these first. Within the lobby of SAP Build choose Control Tower on the left hand. Within the Tenant Configuration you see a tile called Generative AI. If you have chosen it, you will see a toggle to enable it. For more information about availability and licensing, please see here.

 

Creating an SAP Build Apps Frontend Project and Consuming your Backend Data

Back in the lobby, you can now proceed with creating a new SAP Build Apps frontend project.  

 

 

Once in the frontend editor, go to the Integrations tab.

 

Choose your desired backend by, e.g., SAP S/4HANA public cloud edition. Choose Install Integration on the top right and afterwards enable the data entity by choosing the button which appears afterwards under the Install Integration button.

 

Create pages using AI

Once installed and enabled, a new button called Generate Pages will be shown. This button is powered by AI and creates the relevant CRUD (create, read, update, delete) pages for you.

 

After a few seconds all relevant pages are automatically generated. Choose open pages view.

 

You will get forwarded to the frontend editor in which you see the overview of all pages. To view the page in detail, choose the relevant page, e.g., edit products.

 

Preview your application

To test your application, click Save, and then choose the Preview button.

The generated pages and application can now be easily enhanced based on your needs. Simply modify the generated content, copy and paste the pages, and add additional fields as you desire.

 

Current quota limits for this functionality

 
Currently, we do have a quota limit based on 100 requests / user / month. Please have a look into this documentation in case of any updates.

 

For more information

Unlock new levels of productivity with AI in SAP Build

Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - adding S/4HANA Cloud

Get started with SAP Build Apps with an SAP BTP Trial Account

entry.title: Re: Negative value not allowed for depreciation area 93
entry.content: Hi, Area 93 is typically a derived area made up from ((Area 92) minus (Area 01)). It isn't possible in standard to have area 93 go below zero by any setting in that area, but on their own responsibility they could try on their test system some examples setting negative values allowed on area 01 and area 92 to see if that gives them the expected results. Keep in mind this is out of line with the intentions of area 93 which is delivered for compliance reasons. If they make these changes they must be willing to accept responsibility for any unforeseen results. Kind regards Mark

entry.title: Email to Case Party Assignment with Custom Code on Service Cloud V2
entry.content:
Email Channels are used in Sales and Service Cloud Version 2 to manage two-way email communication between customers and the organization.

This allows the system to recognize the customer based on the sender’s email address and perform the necessary party and case type assignments. These assignments can be done using either Business Rules or Custom Code Extensions.

For detailed steps on how to create an Email Channel, you can refer to the following SAP Note:
https://me.sap.com/notes/0003247300

In this blog, we will define Case Parties by adding a Custom Code Extension to the Email Channel.

Step 1: Creating the Configuration

1.1 Go to Settings → Integration → Communication Configuration and click the New Configuration button.

1.2 Assign a name and select the communication system. Value mapping is not required for this example.

1.3 Scroll down to the HTTP Data section and click on the Search and Add button.From the list, find and add "Email to Case: Determine Parties."

1.4 Define a path for your Communication System, then click Save and Activate to complete the process. Your Communication Configuration is now created.

Step 2: Adding the Configuration to the Email Channel

2.1 Select the Email Channel where you want to add the configuration, then click the Edit button at the bottom of the page.
2.2 In the Determination Rules section, enable Party Determination From Custom Code Extensions, and save your changes. This adds the configuration to the selected channel.

Note: You must activate this setting individually for each channel. You can distinguish between channels using the emailAddress field in the event.

 

Step 3: Event Analysis and Required Response Format

3.1 Event Structure

When you set up the communication system connection and send an email to the defined channel address, the system automatically identifies an active contact—if one exists.
In that case, the account and contact objects are added to the event. If no matching contact exists in the system, these objects are not included.

{ "subject": "Mail Subject",
"origin": "EMAIL",
"channel": {
"id": "fab75ee7-50c7-11ef-8827-579205a08065",
"name": "Channel Name",
"emailAddress": "{$channelEmailAddress}",
},
"caseType": "Z_Type", "isRecommendedCaseType": false,
"account": {
"id": "11ee82f8-020d-29de-afdb-816187020a00",
"isDeleted": false,
"isMain": false,
"partyType": "ACCOUNT"
},
"contact": {
"id": "11ee4bca-2589-137e-afdb-818517020a00",
"isDeleted": false,
"isMain": false,
"partyType": "CONTACT"
},
"description": { "content": "Mail Subject", "noteId": "9d75e981-08ad-11f0-a71f-652ded6ef860", "noteType": "S001" },
"relatedObjects": [ { "objectId": "9c89b4d7-08ad-11f0-b495-c10c83bb41b2", "type": "39",  "role": "1" } ],
"timePoints": { "reportedOn": "2025-03-24T12:42:03Z" },
"emailInteraction": {
"id": "9c89b4d7-08ad-11f0-b495-c10c83bb41b2",
"senderEmailAddress": "{$senderEmailAddress}",
"toRecipients" : [ "$channelEmailAddress}" ]
},
"isLastResponseByCustomer": true}

3.2 Response Structure

For example, suppose you want to assign emails from the domain sapservicecloud.com to a dummy account. This can be achieved using the following Node.js code blocks.

Since UUIDs may differ across tenants, it is not recommended to use fixed values. Instead, handle them dynamically. Alternatively, you can detect the tenant and prepare a conditional structure to avoid delays caused by additional API calls.

module.exports = {
  main: async function (event) {
      console.log("Incoming Event: ",event.data);
      let payload = {};
      const eventData = event.data;
      const senderEmail = eventData.emailInteraction.senderEmailAddress;
      if(senderEmail.endsWith("@sapservicecloud.com")){
        payload.accountId = {$exampleUUID};
      }
      return payload;
  }
}

Below are the parties and their corresponding field names to be used in the response payload:

  • Individual Customer → individualCustomerId
  • Supplier → supplierId
  • Account → accountId
  • Employee → employeeId
  • Contact → contactId
  • Supplier Contact → supplierContactId

Add these to the payload in your response. If everything is configured correctly, you should see your email in the Case list, associated with the specified account.

If the case is not created, go to Settings → Cases → Case Auto Creation Monitoring.
Find your email and check the error message. Click the ID to view the detailed error information.

entry.title: Re: How to create a line chart in R3
entry.content:
Bom dia
De nada! Foi um prazer ajudar 🚀O tipo chart_sdata não existe diretamente porque é uma classe local. Quais são as alternativas possíveis? ‌ ‌🚀

🚀

🔹um tipo global no dicionário de dados (SE11)

Se você precisar reutilizar essa estrutura em vários programas, uma abordagem mais robusta é criar um tipo de estrutura global (TYPES ou TABLE TYPE) no Data Dictionary (SE11). Desta forma, qualquer programa pode acessá-lo.

Passos:

 

  • Ir para a transação SE11

  • Selecione 'Tipo de Dados'

  • Crie um novo tipo de estrutura com os mesmos campos que chart_sdata

  • Salvar e ativar

  • Use o novo tipo no seu código:

    abap
    Copiar Editar
     

    TIPOS: INÍCIO DE ty_chart_sdata,
    campo1 TIPO char20,
    campo2 TIPO int4,
    FIM DE ty_chart_sdata.

    DADOS: lt_chart TIPO TABELA DE ty_chart_sdata.

    Isso torna sua estrutura acessível globalmente.

     

    🔹uma classe global para o tipo

    Se você quiser encapsular uma estrutura dentro de uma classe, mas mantê-la reutilizável, você pode criar uma classe global no SE24 (ABAP Class Builder) e definir uma seção TYPES dentro dela:

    abap
    Copiar Editar
     
    CLASSE zcl_chart_data DEFINIÇÃO PÚBLICA FINAL.
    SEÇÃO PÚBLICA.
    TIPOS: INÍCIO DE sdata,
    campo1 TIPO char20,
    campo2 TIPO int4,
    FIM DE sdata.
    TIPOS: tt_sdata TIPO TABELA DE sdata.
    FIM DA CLASSE.
    Agora, em qualquer programa, você pode referenciá-lo assim:
    DADOS: lt_chart TIPO zcl_chart_data=>tt_sdata.
    Espero que esta dica seja útil!

    Você já tem o "não", vá atrás do "sim" — apenas 🚀🚀🚀

entry.title: External Service Management vs Lean Service Procurement in S4 HANA
entry.content:
1. External Services Management

External services management (ESM) incorporates functionality that is relevant to the procurement and execution of services at a company. Services can be planned using a service master record or via free text, or they can be unplanned, which means that, instead of referencing a service master, they reference a monetary limit for the services performed. The item category "D" (Service) is used for external service procurement in SAP MM. It helps differentiate services from materials and facilitates the handling of service-related processes including service entry sheet and invoicing.

Note: You can also Item Category U in PR/PO and during SES, you can maintain the account assignment details.

Configuration:

The Service Category differentiates between different types of services. This can be done by following the navigation path Materials Management > External Services Management > Service Master >Define Service Category.  The Org. Serv. Status can be AS03 which has both Basic Data and Purchasing Data Status.

Defining and Assigning activity numbers by following the navigation path Materials Management > External Services Management > Service Master > Define Number Ranges.

Service Master Record

A service master record can be entered using Transaction AC03. In addition, SERV Service: purchasing has been selected as the Service Category. The Mat/Srv.Grp (material/services group) is maintained along with Base Unit of Measure, and the Valuation class is maintained as 3200 (services).

Conditions in External Service Management

Conditions are found in ESM similar to those found in normal purchasing. Conditions apply to services such as discounts, surcharges, and taxes. One method of entering a condition for a service is to enter a total price condition using Transaction ML33 (Activity along with Vendor, Plant and P.Org).

Procurement of Services

Services can be purchased using Transaction ME21N (Create Purchase Order). In addition to the maintaining the Vendor, Purch. Organization, Purchasing Group, and Delivery date, the Account Assignment needs to be maintained as K (for cost center), and Item Category needs to be set as D.

We maintained Planned Services with Service Master/ free Text in the Sub Service Section and for Unplanned Services, we maintained the Overall and Expected value.

When a supplier has completed a service, or even partially performed a service, this information can be entered into the SAP system. Delivered or performed services are recorded on the service entry sheet, which can accommodate both planned and unplanned services in ML81N Transaction.

If we add any material that was consumed or used during the service period, we can maintain the same in the PO as separate line item. However, we cannot do SES using ML81N for the material. Instead, the only way is to do a separate Goods Receipt using MIGO.

2. Lean Services Procurement in S4 HANA

Lean services is newly introduced process in SAP S/4HANA to simplify service procurement by allowing service entry sheet for both materials consumed during services as well as service line items in the PO.  A new material type, "SERV," is used to identify services, enabling users to create material master for services without relying on item category "D" in purchasing documents. 

Creating Item Hierarchies (Optional)

An item hierarchy is a means of grouping items (services or materials) in purchasing documents. An item hierarchy can consist of one or multiple item sets with subitems. This way you can structure and sort your items. First, we need to enable item hierarchies in purchasing using the path SPRO > Material Management > Purchasing > Environment Data > Activate Item Hierarchies in Purchasing. By default, this configuration step is switched off.

Note that you cannot deactivate the step once you have switched it on. Once enabled, you can use item hierarchies for purchase contracts, purchase orders, purchase requisitions, and the app Manage Model Product Specifications is used to create and activate the Item Hierarchies.

Note: The Services should have Product Type Group as 02 and Consumable Materials should have Product Type group as 01. You can only record materials that were planned together with a service in a purchase order. This means that you have to maintain at least one item of product type group Service (02) in a service entry sheet. If you only add material items from the purchase order to the service entry sheet, an error occurs, and the service entry sheet can't be posted.

Other General Information:

You can’t record the following material types in a service entry sheet:

  • batch-managed materials
  • materials with classification
  • materials with serial numbers
  • materials with split valuation
  • materials with manufacturer part numbers

If the service entry sheet item refers to a purchase order limit item (E- Enhanced Limit) (unplanned services), only single account assignment or Unknown account assignment is possible. However, purchase order (standard) item are also capable of multiple account assignment distributed by amount, quantity and percentage.

Procurement of Lean Services

We have created a PO with following line items:

Planned Services

  1. Service Code with SERV Material Type which as Product Type Group as 02. Here Acc. Assignment is K and Item Category is Standard.
  2. Service Free Text with Product Type Group as 02. Here Acc. Assignment is K and Item Category is Standard.
  3. Material Code with Product Type Group as 01. Here Acc. Assignment is K and Item Category is Standard. Materials that are inventoried i.e Blank Account Assignment cannot be processed via Lean Service Procurement.
  4. Material Free Text with Product Type Group as 01. Here Acc. Assignment is K and Item Category is Standard.

Unplanned Services

  1. Material / Service Free Text with Item Category E and Acc. Assignment K is maintained. The Product Type Group can be 01 or 02.

We can do SES for the above cases only (with exception if item 60) via Manage Sevice Entry Sheet - Lean Services app and not via ML81N. 

Configuration:

For the material type SERV, maintain the Product Type Group as 2 using the following path IMG > Logistics General > Material Master > Basic Settings > Material Types > Define Attributes of Material Types

We need to allow Item category E - Enhanced Limit for PR and PO using the path IMG > Materials Management > Purchasing > Purchase Order > Define Document Types

We need to set Field Selection for Purchase orders for fields like Start/End date for Services, product Type Group, Value Limit Fields etc. using the path IMG > Materials Management > Purchasing > Purchase Requisition > Define Screen Layout at Document Level

3. Blanket Purchasing

A blanket PO is used when a company needs to purchase low-value services or materials at minimal cost. By reducing the effort needed by the purchasing department, you can more economically monitor the transaction. For example, let’s say your company supplies its offices with general cleaning and janitorial supplies, ordered on a biweekly or monthly basis. This company should use a blanket PO rather than creating a separate PO each time they need supplies. When the material is consumed or services are performed, an invoice is posted in the system with reference to the PO, and no goods receipts or service entry sheets are posted in the system.

  • Blanket POs are valid for the long term; therefore, you don’t need to create them every time you reorder supplies.
  • A goods receipt or service entry sheet maintenance isn’t required.
  • Material master records aren’t required.
  • The Document Type for a blanket order is FO rather than the normal document type for POs, which is usually NB.
  • The item category in a blanket order is B for a limit order. As a result, the PO will be created with a limit value and not a line item, as shown under the Limits tab in the lower half of the screen.
  • In Transaction MIRO, click the Account Assignment button and, in the popup that appears, enter details such as the G/L account and the cost center to which these materials or services should be charged.
  • When creating a blanket PO, the account assignment will be initially maintained as unknown (U). Therefore, you’ll need to maintain the account assignment details when posting an invoice for the blanket PO. To maintain account assignment details in invoice verifications (Transaction MIRO), use configuration Transaction OME9 and choose Account Assignment U. In the detailed screen, select the AA Chgable at IR checkbox.

Sources : https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/af9ef57f504840d2b81be8667206d485/4ac8acf820ad41a8a5841420085ba68d.html?locale=en-US&version=2022.002

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/service-procurement-external-service-management-amp-lean-services-with/ba-p/13989897

entry.title: The "Import Sales Orders" Fiori app in SAP S/4HANA allows users to create sales orders from an Excel
entry.content:
The "Import Sales Orders" Fiori app in SAP S/4HANA allows users to create sales orders from an Excel file efficiently. Here’s how you can use it:

Steps to Create Sales Orders from a File in S/4HANA using the Fiori App

  1. Open the Fiori App
  • Log in to your SAP Fiori Launchpad.
  • Search for the app "Import Sales Orders" (App ID: F4829).
  • Click to open it.

  1. Download the Template
  • Click on "Download Template" to get the required file format.
  • The template contains necessary fields like Sold-to Party, Material, Quantity, Delivery Date, etc.

 

  1. Fill in the Sales Order Data
  • Open the downloaded Excel template.
  • Enter sales order details, ensuring all mandatory fields are correctly filled.

  1. Upload the File
  • Go back to the Fiori app and click "Upload".
  • Select your completed Excel file.

  1. Validate & Process
  • The system will validate the data and highlight any errors.
  • Fix any errors and re-upload if necessary.
  1. Create Sales Orders
  • Once the data is correct, click "Import" to create sales orders in the system.
  • The system will process the file, and successful orders will be created.

 

  1. Monitor & Check Sales Orders
  • You can review created sales orders using VA03 (Display Sales Order) in SAP GUI or the Fiori app "Manage Sales Orders".

This method is useful for bulk sales order creation without development effort. Let me know if you need troubleshooting tips or further details! 🚀

entry.title: Sub Bins or Bin sections in EWM complete configuration and step by step process
entry.content:
In this document following scenarios are covered.

  1. Put away for external procurement
  2. Transfer from another Storage type
  3. Stock removal from sub bins
  4. Initial stock upload in sub bins.

 

 

Sub bins means subdividing a storage bin, primarily used for pallet storage where the system creates multiple sub-bins within a main storage bin based on handling unit (HU) type, especially when pallet storage is defined as the storage behavior.

This works only with Pallet storage in which system allow to store different HU types in a single storage bin section. Main storage bin is divided into several smaller Sub bins / bin sections and this strategy enables Put away activity for different HU types into Sub bins.
This strategy Defines number of quants for each combination of storage bin type and HU type.

The use of pallet storage offers significant benefits in warehouse operations, including optimized space utilization, easier identification of inventory, and efficient handling of palletized goods and stock. 

 

Configuration.

  1. Storage Type:

In storage type you need to select Storage behavior as Pallet storage .

The following figure shows the "Pallet Storage by HU Type" strategy. For example, a storage bin can accommodate multiple pallets depending on the size of the pallet, such as three europallets (80 x 120) or two industrial pallets (100 x 120). A storage bin may be able to accommodate a single oversized pallet or several very narrow pallets.

Path : SPRO-->Extended Warehouse Management --> Master Data --> Storage Type

In Storage type configurations , following are mandatory ,

  1. Storage type - 1. Pallet Storage
  2. HU Requirement – X- HU Required.
  3. HU Type check - Check the box
  4. No Goods Issue - Check the box

 

  1. Define Bin Sections

Storage bin section Key is configured to determine how many Handling unit’s of different HU types can be stored in a bin. The storage bin section is a key which is alphanumeric and it is  configured with no. of HU’s which can be put away.

The storage behavior Pallet Storage takes into consideration the fact that many high rack storage areas are set up in such a way, that a shelf section can take on differing numbers of pallets. The system can process different HU Types (such as euro pallets) and can assign these to a section of the shelf. Mixing of different HU types (pallet types) within a shelf section is not possible.

A shelf section is mapped in the system as a main storage bin. During the first putaway to the main bin, the system creates the number and type of sub-bins you specified in your definition.

The capacity check during putaway always refers to the capacity in the main bin.

You define a section of main bins into sub-bins.

  • You enter a description for the section.
  • You specify the number and type of the sub-bins.
  • You specify the description suffix for the sub-bins. The description of the sub-bins consists of: <Name of the main bin> + "/" + "description suffix according to section".

Example

Name of the main bin:

A-01-01-01

Defined suffixes in      

section:

1 2 3

Name of sub-bins:

A-01-01-01/1

 

A-01-01-01/2

 

A-01-01-01/3

 

Path : SPRO--> Extended warehouse management --> Good Receipt process --> Strategies --> Put away Rules --> Storage behavior : Pallets --> Define Bin Sections

Max. no. of HUs : If you define three bin sections and set the maximum number of handling units to three, then only one handling unit can be stored within one bin section.

There are three bin sections, and you change the maximum number of handling units from three to nine. Then, three handling units can be stored within one bin section.

 

 

  1. Perform bin sectioning per for bin type and HU type Specify the section to be used for each Storage type, Storage Bin Type and HU Type for the put away behavior "Pallet". During put away, the system uses the HU type for the HU to put away and the bin type of the found (empty) main storage bin, to find a storage bin section.

             Assign the sections you have defined to the required storage bin types and HU types

Path : SPRO-->Extended warehouse management --> Good Receipt process --> Strategies --> Put away Rules --> Storage behavior : Pallets --> Perform Bin sectioning for Bin type and HU type

Other Prerequisites required are;

 

Assignment of Storage bin type to Storage type, Handling unit type for each Storage type & HU type for each storage bin type.

 

Assignment of Storage bin type to Storage type

 

Handling unit type for each Storage type

HU type for each storage bin type.

Product master

Test Case 1

For external procurement

Create PO then create Inbound delivery , then Go to /N/SCWM/PRDI to create task

Create HU and move the product to HU for packing .

Create a Task

In destination bin Z900_PL90_01 automatically sub bin is created and displayed as Z900_PL90_01/1 as per the configuration settings.

Now Stock is available in Sub bins. You can check it in Monitor screen -  /N/SCWM/MON .

In the Storage Bin , Bin section tab is getting updated with Bin position details as follows .

As per the configuration Storage bin type Z002 allows 3 Sub bins , hence it created 3 Sub bin and for two its showing empty and Z900_PL90_01/1 is showing with stock and No. of HU is showing as 1.

Display  Sub bin Z900_PL90_01/1

In Tab Bin Sectioning  -  Bin Section , Max. No. of HUs and NO. of HUs are getting updated.

Test case 2

Stock transfer from another Storage type to Palletize Storage Type .

Move the stock from Storage type HZ90 normal bin Z900_HZ90_03 to storage type PL90  which is having Sub bins and check confirm Task check box.

 

Now the Stock available in Storage type  HZ90 with HU 1029 is moved to PL 90 and created new Sub bin

Now check the Storage bin , No. of HU is 2 and Sub Bin Z900_PL90_01/02 not empty.

Test case 3 ,

Removing Stock from Sub bin .

Transfer stock from PL90 Z900_PL90_01/1 to  Storage type  HZ90 with HU 1063 .

Stock is moved to HZ90

 

In Display Storage bin  the Source Bin Z900_PL90_01 , Sub Bin Z900_PL90_01/1 should  show as empty.

Test case 4 : Initial Stock Upload with Sub bins

 

For Initial stock upload in sub bin use below template with csv format  in T code  /N/SCWM/ISU.

 

POSTYPEMATNROWNEROWNER_ROLEBATCHCATSTOCK_DOCCATSTOCK_DOCNOSTOCK_ITMNOSTOCK_USAGEENTITELEDENTITLED_ROLECOOQUANUNITHUTYPLGPLAGR_DATEGR_TIMEVFDATPMATEXTNOHUIDENTPARHUIDENTTOPHUIDENTROWREFROW
1              Z900Z900_PL90_01/2  700011 1111 
4TEST123190000000BP F2    190000000BP 2PC Z900_PL90_01/2    11121
1              Z900Z900_PL90_01/1  700011 2223 
4TEST1234190000000BP F2    190000000BP 4PC Z900_PL90_01/1    22243
                           
  190000000                        

After executing this , tasks  are created and confirmed and HUs are created.

 

Check the T code /N/SCWM/MON  to verify the uploaded stock.

Check Bin Stock for sub bins , HUs  to confirm stock uploaded in correct Sub bins.

entry.title: External Numbering in RAP.
entry.content:
Hi all.

Here, the consumer hands over the primary key values for the CREATE operation. 

In the SAP Restful Application Programming Model (RAP), numbering plays a crucial role in uniquely identifying business objects. By default, RAP supports system-generated (internal) numbering, where the system assigns a unique key automatically. However, certain business scenarios require external numbering, where the key is predefined by the user or follows a specific business logic

As the value for the key field is passed by the consumer, the uniqueness has to be checked explicitly. 

step1

Basic view for external number.  

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE] 
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED 
@EndUserText.label: 'basic view for external numbering' 
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 
@ObjectModel.usageType:{ 
serviceQuality: #X, 
sizeCategory: #S, 
dataClass: #MIXED 
} 
define root view entity zi_fligtht as select from zgiri_t_sfligth 
{ 

key carrier_id as CarrierId, 
name as Name, 
currency_code as CurrencyCode, 
is_complete as IsComplete, 
no_of_connections as NoOfConnections, 
local_created_by as LocalCreatedBy, 
local_created_at as LocalCreatedAt, 
local_last_changed_by as LocalLastChangedBy, 
local_last_changed_at as LocalLastChangedAt, 
last_changed_at as LastChangedAt 
}

Database table. 

Draft table.

step 2

Consumption view for external numbering. 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED 
@EndUserText.label: 'consumption view for external numbering' 
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 
@ObjectModel.semanticKey: [ 'CarrierId' ] 
@UI : { headerInfo: { 
typeName: 'carrier', 
typeNamePlural: 'carrier', 
title: { 
type: #STANDARD, 
value: 'Name' 
}, 
description: { 
value: 'CarrierId' 
} 
} 
} 
define root view entity zc_flight_external provider contract transactional_query as projection on zi_fligtht 
{ 
@UI.facet: [{ 
id: 'CarrierInfo', 
label: 'Carrier Details', 
type:#COLLECTION , 
position: 10 
},{ 
label: 'Carrier', 
id: 'Carrier', 
type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE, 
purpose: #STANDARD, 
parentId: 'CarrierInfo', 
position: 20 
} 
] 
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 10 }] 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 10 }] 
key CarrierId, 
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 20 }] 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 20 }] 
Name, 
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 30 }] 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 30 }] 
CurrencyCode, 
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 40 }] 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 40 }] 
LastChangedAt 
}

 

Step 3

Behavior definition for for basic view . 

managed implementation in class zbp_i_fligtht unique; 
strict ( 2 ); 
with draft; 
define behavior for zi_fligtht //alias <alias_name> 
persistent table zgiri_t_sfligth 
draft table zgiri_d_sfligth 
lock master 
total etag LastChangedAt 
authorization master ( instance ) 
//etag master <field_name> 
{ 
create; 
update; 
delete; 
field ( mandatory ) Name, CurrencyCode; 
field (readonly : update) CarrierId; 
field (readonly) LastChangedAt;
draft action edit; 
draft action activate; 
draft action discard; 
draft action resume; 
draft determine action prepare; 
mapping for zgiri_t_sfligth 
{ 
CarrierId = carrier_id; 
Name = name; 
CurrencyCode = currency_code; 
LastChangedAt = last_changed_at; 
} 
}

Step 4

Behavior definition for consumption view. 

projection; 
strict ( 2 ); 
use draft; 
define behavior for zc_flight_external alias carrier //alias <alias_name> 
{ 
use create; 
use update; 
use delete; 
use action edit; 
use action activate; 
use action discard; 
use action resume; 
use action prepare; 
}

Step 5

Service definition  

@EndUserText.label: 'service for external numbering'
define service Zi_fligtht_ser10 {
  expose zc_flight_external;
}

step 6

service binding

 

Output. 

Once we click on create you will get on pop up for enter key value. 

Once you entered id if again if you want to enter same id it will through an error it will validate here itself . 

 

Conclusion.

Here we can give our own primary key as per our requirement.

External numbering in the SAP Restful Application Programming Model (RAP) allows business applications to use predefined keys instead of system-generated numbers when creating entities. This approach is crucial for maintaining consistency with business rules and legacy system identifiers, such as material numbers, customer IDs, or invoice numbers. While it provides flexibility and control, it also requires additional validation to ensure uniqueness and prevent conflicts.

entry.title: "Build Better Dashboards" - Process
entry.content:
Please note that this article is part of a Dashboard Design Best-Practices blog series,
created to assist you in designing and building meaningful dashboards with SAP Analytics Cloud.
Click here to open the Dashboard Design Best-Practices blog series hub page in a new window

 

Intro

You want to build better dashboards but aren't sure where to begin? Here´s our suggestion.

Focus your attention on the 'design-led development' process, often successfully implemented in collaborative environments where end users, designers and development teams work hand in hand, can support your efforts.
By leveraging proven design thinking methods, this approach ensures an optimal user experience and the successful coverage of user needs.

Design-led development - process

 

Blog Content 

We focus on the following 3 main steps of the process

1. Discover Phase
Understand the needs of your end users and turn this information into insights by working
step by step through the user research process.

2. Design Phase
Translate insights and results from the user research phase into a design proposal

3. Develop Phase
Implement the final results from the design phase into predefined SAC templates
and learn how to customize and extend the template to meet the needs of your individual app.

 

1. Discover Phase

The discover phase (also known as User Research) focuses on understanding customer and end-user needs through on-site visits, interviews, and observations. The goal is to comprehend business roles, tasks, pain points, and the sequence of activities. Documented insights inform the design phase, ensuring a high-quality user experience and benefiting both the product and end users.

 

Focusing On the User

What Are the Benefits?
There are some clear advantages of putting the end user at the center of an analytic product development from the start, and therefore tailoring the product to their needs.

  • First, and most important, focusing on the user helps the project team to gain an understanding of the necessary insights (context of use, user needs, requirements) related to the targeted product. 
    It can also lead to insights that in turn lead to further innovative products.
  • If different opinions regarding functions or content slow progress during the project, the insights gained from the users can help to find a common solution.
  • The user feels like they are taken seriously when they participate in the development of a product.
  • The user is more likely to use the product if their needs are met.
  • If the product fits the user, they don't have to choose another product, which builds brand trust.
  • The development team builds its reputation by developing customer-centric products that
    end-users want to use.
  • If end users are available during the development process, it's easy to validate open questions or changes with them. Ultimately, this saves the company money and time.
 

User Research Process

Preparing Research

Before starting your research, plan your course of action. In the beginning, ask yourself: 

  • What insights am I looking for? 
  • What are the objectives?
  • What questions do I want answer?

    There are several UX methods to gather user requirements and needs, such as user interviews, focus groups, surveys, A/B testing, and desk research. In this blog we´re focus on user interviews.

    Basic Tips for conducting user interviews with End Users:

    • Prepare an interview guide before the interview.
    • Avoid questions that force the user to answer yes or no. Instead, use open-ended questions that start with "when", "how", "where", "why" or "what".
    • To prevent the interviewees from getting confused, explain the purpose of the interview and the related goals at the beginning of the interview.
    • Introduce yourself and others from your side during the beginning of the interview.
    • Ask thoughtful questions that help the interviewees to formulate and clarify their model.
    • Be aware that an interviewee will not always say everything directly, because:
      • The interviewee takes something for granted (even if it is not).
      • The interviewee can't think about everything in the moment.
      • Do not discuss technical feasibility too early, but try to understand the requirement correctly first.
      • Try to find note takers and observers for your interview, instead of conducting it alone. Other people might gather additional information and impressions.

 

Evaluate Research

After conducting your research, evaluate your insights to decide with ones are relevant for your project and will help you during the design phase and later. Synthesis is the process of combining findings and research results into useful information.

Step 1: Extract and Summarize Data
From the interviews conducted, find the relevant information that supports the definition of your product. Write these on sticky notes. The minimal size of the sticky note forces you to reduce the information to the most important parts. Try to use only one sticky note per piece of information.

Step 2: Group the Information
After collecting the information on sticky notes, group similar information/thoughts into a category. Give each category a heading. The headings can be general at the beginning, but the more you iterate across the different clusters, you will gradually discover more specific terminology.

Step 3: Turn "Information" into “Insights”
This is the most interesting, but also the most difficult, step. For this part of the process, the team transforms the collected information into actual insights. It's important to note that the definition of insights can be interpreted very individually. Therefore, try to challenge assumptions or opinions within the team. The team should establish appropriate definitions at this stage.

What might count as insights? Some examples:

  • Commonly known information, or the so-called “must-haves”
  • A new perspective from the user's point of view
  • Needs and problems about which the users themselves were previously unaware
  • How the end user really thinks or behaves
  • Insights that can influence design decisions
  • Findings that can be implemented currently versus ideas for future releases

 

2. Design Phase

Equipped with findings from the discover phase, the design phase can start, resulting in an initial prototype validated by end users. By the end of the design phase, a ready-to-implement design is achieved.

 

 

 

After gathering insights and results from the user research phase, the team should work together to translate the results into an initial design proposal. This can be implemented at various levels of detail. We recommend at least a "low-fidelity" Mock-Up.

Low-Fidelity Mock-Up
A low-fi (low fidelity) mock-up is a visualization that focuses mainly on the content, the structure, and the individual required steps of an application/dashboard. The result should be simple and without high-level details. This type of prototype offers advantages in internal team communication and allows for easy validation of design ideas with end users. You can either draw a mock-up by hand on paper, draw it digitally, or use the  Figma Library for schematic dashboard visualization.

 

Gather Iterative Feedback, Decide, and Adjust
Creating low-fi mock-ups is subject to an iterative process. This means constant validation of feedback and decisions with the end users involved. With the help of a mock-up, it is easy to implement corresponding adjustments at any time. This ensures that the end result is a construct that meets the needs and requirements of the user. Ultimately, after the last round of validation with the user and the team, the final mock-up can then be implemented directly in SAC without the need to create another pixel-perfect high-fidelity prototype.

Mock-Up Drawn Using Digital Device
Low-fi mock-ups drawn by hand or digitally allow for quick and easy creation and immediate adjustments or refinements to the design, as well as rapid idea generation.

 

Mock-Up Created with Figma
Low-fi mock-ups created with the Figma UI Kit streamline the design process by providing ready-to-use components, ensuring a consistent and professional look for the mock-ups, while allowing for interaction.

 

Additional Ressources
(click links to open the information in a new browser tab)

  • Visualizing Data Overview
  • Dashboard Design Patterns
  • (tbd) Figma UI Kit (Figma License necessary)

 

3. Develop Phase 

Now it's time to implement the final design proposal in SAC.
To support you in this phase, you'll find the necessary
information in the links below.
(click links to open the information in a new browser tab)

 

Use the latest SAP Analytics Cloud Template and Fiori Horizon Theme:
SAP Analytics Cloud - Template & Theme

Learn how to customize and extend the template to meet the needs of your individual Insight application
SAP Analytics Cloud - best practices 

Go to the Overview page of this blog series.
Dashboard Design - Best Practices

entry.title: Visualizing Data with SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Please note that this article is part of a Dashboard Design Best-Practices blog series, created to assist you in designing and building meaningful dashboards with SAP Analytics Cloud. Click here to open the Dashboard Design Best-Practices blog series hub page in a new window

Content
This blog page focus on the art of visualizing data through the selection of appropriate
chart types to convey the intended message.

  1. Comparison
  2. Time-Analysis
  3. Part-to whole
  4. Cumulation
  5. Deviation
  6. Distribution
  7. Correlation

 

First, let’s start with some general rules of thumb and quotas that you may consider when creating analytical content.

  • Show the necessary information comprehensively.
  • Everything on the screen has to make sense.
  • Support data comparison capabilities.
  • Everything that belongs together is also grouped visually like this.
  • Show variances.
  • Use graphics whenever possible.
  • Use consistent terms.
  • Group with empty space.
  • Clear support of the information hierarchy.
  • Time-series are always on the x-axies
  • The most important information is visually highlighted.
  • Clear info where the action is actually needed.
  • Aesthetically pleasing, clear visual design.
  • Avoid abbreviations (unless these are colloquial).
  • Do not use semantic colors in the design, e.g. green, yellow and red (exception if it's company branding).
  • Lead with purpose (guide the user to the appropriate actions).

Quotes

  • „The pieces on the dashboard have to be arranged in relation to one another“ - Stephan View
  • "Overview first, zoom and filter, then details-on-demand"
    -> Visual information seeking mantra by Ben Shneiderman" 
  • "Perfection is achieved, not when there is nothing more to add,
    but when there is nothing left to take away"
    ->     Antoine de Saint-Exupéry from "The little Prince"
  • "Less is faster" - Hick´s Law

 

Chart Types

Chart types are important tools for data visualization because they help transform raw data into visual formats that are easy to understand, analyze, and communicate. Choosing a chart type depends on the type of data you're working with and the message you want to convey.

 

1. Comparison (Structure Analysis)

Choose Bar and Column Charts as the most effective visualization for structure analysis, also called nominal comparison or category comparison. 

Note:
When showing development over time, use always charts with horizontal category axes (Column Chart)
This is one of the most well-known guidelines established by IBCS (International Business Communication Standards) in its practical guidelines for the consistent design of reports.

The also propose the usage of semantic patterns to visually distinguish different types of values ​​when comparing them at a glance.

  • Actual Values: what values are (solid pattern with dark color)
  • Previous Year Values: what values were (solid pattern with neutral grey color)
  • Forecast Values: what values might be (hatch pattern)
  • Plan Values: what values should be (empty pattern)

 

2. Time Analysis 

Use a line chart if you want to emphasize the trend over time.

 

Use a column chart if you want to emphasize the values themselves.

Note that there're other time-related categories that have an intrinsic order and indicate progression or trend, such as age and age group

 

3. Part-to whole

When thinking about visualizing parts of a whole, you are likely to turn to pie or donut chart.
However, in many cases bar chart is a far better option because you can:

  • Compare parts to each other.
  • Display category labels and value labels associated with each part.
  • Display multiple values.
  • Display small values in a better way.
  • Cut down on the usage of colors and corresponding legends you have to use.

Part-to-Whole Analysis by Bar Chart

 

Part-to-Whole Analysis by Stacked Percentage Bar Chart

 

Donut Chart

Donut charts work best with a small number of categories (around 5-7).
Too many categories can make the chart hard to interpret (see Pic. 1).

Note that when using pie or donut charts, you should be careful when enabling the "Top N" options, as this will change the original percentage values ​​and recalculate each segment's contribution to the total of the "Top N" categories (see Pic. 2).

Instead, use the group function in SAC to create a segment that represents the remaining (Pic. 3)
How to activate the grouping function in SAC can be found here (Link opens in a new browser tab)

 

 

Tree Map

Tree map is a good visualization to present the hierarchical data, with nested figures rendered by rectangles of different sizes.

In the example below, each rectangle signifies a department and its size is in proportion to the number of department employees. You can use colors to differentiate employee genders, but here the chart only displays female employee's number. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

4. Cumulation

You can use waterfall chart to analyze cumulative value. A waterfall chart accumulates successive values and shows how the cumulative value changes from an initial state to a final one.

Variance waterfall chart in vertical orientation displays the variance between different scenarios in structure.
In the example below, the variance waterfall chart shows the net sales in Europe from previous year (PY) to this year (AC). You can see that this year's net sales is 14 million USD higher than the previous year and the difference in Spain is the largest.

 

Variance waterfall chart in horizontal orientation displays the variances across different time periods.In the example below, you can see that shipping cost keeps growing from Q1 to Q4 in the whole year. Q4 is expanded to show changes in months.

 

5. Deviation

Deviation charts, also known as variance charts, are used to visually display how a value deviates from a baseline or expected target.
They are particularly helpful for understanding the differences between actual results and a reference point, such as a goal, target, or historical data. 

 

Actual and Plan Values and Deviation in Bar Charts Side by Side

 

Numeric and Percentage Deviation in Table with In-Cell Charts

 

Focus on Trend
If you want to focus on the trend of the variation, you can use a line chart as the examples below.

 

6. Distribution

Histogramm
A histogram displays the numbers of data points that lie within respective ranges, which is extremely helpful in emphasizing data frequency. In the left example, the axis displays age groups, and the columns show the numbers of employees that fall in different age ranges.

 

Column Chart
If your use case is to display the distribution of employees of each age group in percentage,
a column chart is a good option.

 

Stacked Column Chart
Distribution of Two Series by Stacked Column Chart.


You can use a frequency polygon with multiple lines when you want to compare the distribution of multiple sets of values.

 

Box plot
A Box Plot is an ideal option for displaying the concentration of data, which is constructed from minimum, first quartile, median, third quartile and maximum.

 

7. Correlation

Scatterplot and bubble chart are the most frequently used chart types to visualize correlation,
though line chart or bar chart side by side is also an acceptable solution.

entry.title: SQL Anywhere 17 file does not exist error
entry.content: We have SQL Central 17.0.0.1359 and have deployed at multiple client sites.  One client is running into a new issue.  A quote gets mysteriously added to the front of the File name in the service and this stops the service.  The File Name parm doesn't allow quotes to be added anywhere in the file name when editing this field manually.  What is adding the quote before the file name?  The client had previously removed spaces in the file name so the path was originally C:\Program Files\SQLAnywhere17\Bin64\mlsrv17.exe due to a virus scan that flagged the spaces.  We had them uninstall/reinstall SQL Server 17 and the problem still occurs as seen in the screen shot.

entry.title: SAP IBP Configuration - Configure and Use Change History in SAP IBP
entry.content:
In SAP IBP, the change history feature allows you to track changes made to key figures over time. This can be particularly useful for auditing and understanding how data has evolved. Here's how you can enable and use this feature:

  1. Enable Change History: First, you need to enable the change history for the planning area and the specific key figures you want to track. This can be done in the configuration settings.

  2. Capture Changes: Once enabled, the system will capture various details about changes, including:

    • Modified by: The user who made the change.
    • Modified date: The date and time when the change was made.
    • Modified value: The new value after the change.
    • Attributes: Any relevant attributes associated with the change.
    • Reason code: The reason for the change, if specified.
    • Comments: Any additional comments provided during the change.

  3. View Change History: You can view the change history in the Change History Analysis app within the SAP IBP web interface. This app allows you to filter changes by various criteria, such as specific key figures, dates, or users. You can also download the change history to a CSV file for further analysis.

  4. Best Practices: Since tracking changes can be performance-intensive, it's recommended to enable this feature only for essential data. Regularly purging old change history data can help manage system performance.

By following these steps, you can effectively monitor and analyze changes to key figures in SAP IBP, ensuring better control and transparency in your planning processes.

1. Configure the change history by selecting the Change History indicator in your planning area and for the CONSENSUSDEMANDQTY key figure, and activate your planning area, if you made a change to they key figure settings.

a. Enable change history in planning area

b. Enable change history in specific key figure

c. Activate planning area.

2. In the Planner Workspace, make changes to the CONSENSUSDEMANDQTY key figure values. Save your changes, assign a reason code of your choice, and enter a comment.

a. Create new planning view with key figure CONSENSUSDEMANDQTY

b. Make changes to the CONSENSUSDEMANDQTY key figure, save, select a reason code and add a comment explaining why the data changes was necessary.

c. Save

3. In the IBP Web UI, read the change history log for your planning area, using the Change History Analysis app.

a. On the SAP Fiori launchpad home page, in the General Planner screen area, choose Change History Analysis.

b. In the Change History Analysis screen, choose planning area, version, key figure, attributes, and time period, then Go, create change history report.

c. Observe that the change history. You can view the previous value, the new value, the change by value, the reason code, and the comment

d. Download the report for further processing.

entry.title: How to post vendor down payment requests (SGL 'F') similar to transaction F-47 with BAPI_ACC_DOCUMEN
entry.content:
Yes, it is possible to post Supplier Down Payment Request (Transaction F-47) via BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST or message type ACC_DOCUMENT.

First, let’s use iDoc message type ACC_DOCUMENT (Basic type ACC_DOCUMENT05) and populate the following segments:

E1BPACHE09 - Header BUS_ACT RFST

USERNAME USER01

HEADER_TXT TEST

COMP_CODE 1000

DOC_DATE 20250321

PSTNG_DATE 20250321

DOC_TYPE UE

E1BPACAP09 - Accounts Payable

ITEMNO_ACC 1

VENDOR_NO 1000123

TAX_CODE D1

SP_GL_IND F

E1BPACCR09 Currency

ITEMNO_ACC 1

CURR_TYPE 00

CURRENCY_ISO EUR

AMT_DOCCUR 100

That's all:

It works the same with BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST

entry.title: Low-Code and No-Code Development with SAP BTP: Empowering Business Users
entry.content:
Low-Code and No-Code Development with SAP BTP: Empowering Business Users

In today's fast-moving digital world, businesses need to innovate quickly. But let’s be honest—traditional software development can be slow, expensive, and requires highly skilled developers. This makes it hard for non-technical users to actively contribute to building solutions. This is where SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) comes in, offering low-code and no-code (LCNC) development tools that allow business users to create applications with little to no coding experience.

What is Low-Code/No-Code Development?

Low-code and no-code platforms simplify application development by using visual, drag-and-drop interfaces instead of complex coding. These platforms help teams prototype quickly, bring ideas to life, and empower business users—sometimes called citizen developers—to take charge of their digital transformation.

How SAP BTP Makes Low-Code/No-Code Development Possible

SAP BTP offers several tools to make development easier for both technical and non-technical users:

1. SAP AppGyver – No-Code App Development

SAP AppGyver is a powerful no-code platform that lets users create web and mobile apps without writing any code. Here’s what makes it stand out:

  • Drag-and-drop UI builder for intuitive app design.

  • Easy integration with SAP and external systems.

  • Pre-built logic components to streamline workflows.

  • Cross-platform deployment, meaning apps work on different devices.

2. SAP Business Application Studio – Low-Code Development

For users who need more flexibility, SAP Business Application Studio provides a low-code development environment, offering:

  • Ready-made templates for SAP Fiori applications.

  • Seamless connection to SAP S/4HANA.

  • Extensibility with JavaScript and SAP UI5.

  • Cloud-native development with multi-environment support.

3. SAP Process Automation – Automate Workflows

SAP Process Automation combines workflow automation and robotic process automation (RPA) to:

  • Eliminate repetitive manual tasks.

  • Digitize approval workflows.

  • Boost productivity with automated processes.

Real-World Use Cases of SAP BTP LCNC Tools

Businesses across different industries are using SAP BTP’s LCNC capabilities to speed up digital transformation. Here are some practical applications:

  • Retail: Creating custom order-tracking apps without IT involvement.

  • Finance: Automating invoice approvals and reducing errors.

  • HR: Developing internal employee self-service portals.

  • Manufacturing: Building predictive maintenance dashboards for machinery.

Why Use Low-Code/No-Code with SAP BTP?

  • Speed & Agility: Faster development cycles mean quicker results.

  • Cost Savings: Reduces reliance on IT and expensive developers.

  • Empower Business Users: Enables domain experts to create solutions tailored to their needs.

  • Seamless SAP Integration: Works effortlessly with SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, and other SAP applications.

Final Thoughts

SAP BTP’s low-code and no-code tools make application development accessible to everyone—not just developers. With these tools, businesses can innovate faster, automate processes, and drive digital transformation without the traditional barriers of coding.

Have you tried building an app using SAP BTP’s low-code/no-code tools? Share your experiences in the comments—we’d love to hear how these solutions are making a difference in your organization!

entry.title: Increasing the number of JMS queues in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
By default the starting amount of JMS queues is 30. You can increase the number up to 100 in a self service.
To save you from waisting time when following the note "3318666 - How to increase the JMS queues or Activate Enterprise Messaging in Cloud Integration / SAP Integration Suite “(Version 5 from 01.02.2024) I will share the steps to increase the amount of JMS queues

Prerequisite: Member of Role Collection “Integration_Provisioner“

Goto Integration Suite à Home à Capabilities …

Select “Manage Capabilities”

Here you can edit the amount of JMS queues up to 100

entry.title: Pipeline Generic Step01 - Inbound Processing for XI [FAILS]
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

The very first call from S4 arrives at the iFlow Pipeline Generic Step01 - Inbound Processing for XI. From S4 it is a proxy call.  

SAP is throwing the following error:

Additional Information
org.apache.camel.component.direct.DirectConsumerNotAvailableException: No consumers available on endpoint: direct://Sender. Exchange[E1546C1E1673B48-0000000000000008]
 
Message doesn't go to the next step.  No configuration is maintained on this iFlow.
 
What is missing in the setup. Thanks in Advance.
 
Thanks, 
SB

entry.title: ABAP BTP Cloud, Download files from ABAP program to local PC
entry.content:
As my ABAP BTP trial account is coming to end I needed to backup of the data that was saved in multiple databases tables. Though you could download the contents of the table using the eclipse data preview it was not suitable for me:

  • My table has fields with string data type and  using eclipse data preview did not download the string fields in correct format as it did not escape the special characters correctly
  • I wanted automatic way of taking regular backups of my data  in multiple tables using a utility program in a single of button.

 In the ABAP BTP cloud environment with Eclipse, there is no known way of downloading a file from an ABAP program to a local PC. The on-premise solutions like using cl_gui_frontend_services do not work in ABAP cloud with Eclipse ADT. The linked ABAP repository builds the functionality in two steps:

  1.  Save the file contents as RAWSTRING in database table zfio_files. 

  2. Use table zfio_files to generate an ABAP RAP based Fiori Elements UI. The Fiori application streams the contents of the file from database on the UI as an attachment from where users can download the file.

Note: The purpose of this blog is not to provide any functionality related to conversion of data into CSV and the demo programs does it for testing purpose only. 

Usage

The code is hosted on https://github.com/ajayrawatsap/ZFIO_UTILS

  • Clone or Download the github repo using abapGit Plugin

  • Store file contents in your ABAP program to database using the provided ABAP method create_files of class ZFIO_FILES_SAVE_TO_DB. Check demo class ZCL_FIO_FILE_SAVE_DEMO which can be run as application with eclipse adt 

TYPES: BEGIN OF sample_row,

          book_name TYPE c LENGTH 128,
          author    TYPE c LENGTH 128,
          rating    TYPE p LENGTH 2 DECIMALS 2,
        END OF sample_row.

 DATA files       TYPE zfio_tt_files.
 DATA sample_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF sample_row.

 sample_data = VALUE #(
     (  book_name = 'The Psychology of Money'  author = 'Morgan Housel'  rating = '4.3' )
     (  book_name = 'The Millionaire Next Door'  author = 'Thomas J. Stanley, William D. Danko'  rating = '4.04' )
     (  book_name = 'Thinking, Fast and Slow'  author = 'Daniel Kahneman'  rating = '4.17' ) ).

 DATA(csv_string) = convert_to_csv( sample_data  ).

 DATA(books_xtring) = cl_abap_conv_codepage=>create_out( codepage = `UTF-8` )->convert( csv_string ).


 files = VALUE #( ( file_mimetype = 'text/csv' file_name = 'books.csv' file_content = books_xtring ) ).
 DATA(file_save_obj) = NEW zfio_files_save_to_db( ).

 TRY.
     file_save_obj->create_files( files = files ).
     out->write( 'Records Updated Use Service Binding ZFIO_UI_FILES_OV4 to Launch the Fiori App' ).
   CATCH zcm_fio_checks INTO DATA(fio_error).

     out->write( fio_error->get_text( ) ).

 ENDTRY.
  • Access the Fiori application to view and download the files using service binding ZFIO_UI_FILES_OV4 .
  • Download the file from Fiori UI by clicking on field Attachment. 

On clicking the attachment icon a file dialogue will appear prompting to save the file to your local PC.

The contents of file are correctly transferred into the downloaded file

Other thoughts:

The functionality has been tested on csv files but it should also work on other file types like photos or pdf as long as you are saving the contents of file in RAWSTRING  format which is hexadecimal format.
